codeEvidence.explanation: The full322 startup path starts microphone capture during application initialization without checking an in-app recording state.
codeEvidence.label: Unconditional microphone startup

confidence.rationale: Direct control-flow review shows unconditional thread startup and capture until shutdown; hardware testing confirmed the microphone path is active when the Vita hardware mute is disabled.

findings.remediation: Start capture only after an explicit in-app recording action, stop it immediately when recording ends, expose a visible capture indicator, and clear captured buffers after use.
remediationTests: Launch and idle on every screen while verifying the microphone input port is closed.
Enter sampler and vocoder recording flows and verify capture starts only after the record action and stops on completion/cancel.
Suspend, resume, and exit during recording and verify the input port closes and buffers are cleared.

rootCause.summary: Microphone lifetime is coupled to application lifetime instead of an explicit user recording state.

findings.summary: The full322 startup path opens the Vita microphone and continuously forwards ambient samples to the proprietary recording callback even when the user has not entered a recording workflow.
findings.title: Application captures microphone audio continuously from launch

codeEvidence.explanation: The suffix is concatenated without normalization or containment; dot segments survive to native fopen.
codeEvidence.label: Unchecked fopen path join

confidence.rationale: The unsafe path construction and shipped fopen import are direct evidence, and real Caustic files serialize absolute paths; an end-to-end benign-sentinel Vita PoC through the stripped parser was not performed.

findings.remediation: Route every filesystem bridge through a single length-checked normalizer that rejects dot-segment escape and unauthorized Vita mount paths, and restrict app0 access to read-only operations.
remediationTests: Reject Android paths containing traversal segments that would escape ux0:/data/CAUSTIC3.
Reject direct Vita paths outside the approved data root while preserving valid Caustic assets.
Verify read, create, append, and truncate modes independently.

rootCause.summary: Prefix replacement is treated as path confinement even though it neither normalizes segments nor verifies the canonical destination remains beneath the allowed root.

findings.summary: The fopen compatibility bridge strips an Android storage prefix and joins the remainder to the Caustic data root without canonicalizing dot segments or enforcing containment.
findings.title: Crafted Caustic paths escape the data root through fopen_soloader

codeEvidence.explanation: The suffix is concatenated without normalization or containment; dot segments survive to native open.
codeEvidence.label: Unchecked open path join

confidence.rationale: The unsafe path construction and shipped open/__open_2 imports are direct evidence, and real Caustic files serialize absolute paths; an end-to-end benign-sentinel Vita PoC through the stripped parser was not performed.

findings.remediation: Use the same centralized, length-checked path-policy helper as fopen; reject traversal, unauthorized mounts, and write access to app0 before invoking native open.
remediationTests: Exercise open and __open_2 with traversal paths for read-only and write flags.
Reject direct Vita paths outside the approved root and all app0 write attempts.
Confirm valid project, preset, sample, skin, save, and export operations still work.

rootCause.summary: The open bridge performs syntactic prefix substitution but does not enforce a filesystem policy on the resulting Vita path.

findings.summary: The open and fortified __open_2 compatibility bridges replace Android storage prefixes without canonicalization or a containment check before native open.
findings.title: Crafted Caustic paths escape the data root through open_soloader
